text.title: requirements-lifecycle Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for requirements-lifecycle as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Only three states: To Do, In Progress, Done. Requirements get conflated with task tickets.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Task workflow ≠ requirement lifecycle. To Do might mean Draft, Proposed, or Approved — ambiguity breaks auditability.

failure-mode.description: Approved state never reached. Proposed requirements sit for months without explicit approval or rejection.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Time-bound Proposed; require approval within 2-4 weeks or escalate. A requirement with no approval date is not tracked.

failure-mode.description: Validation (the question "right thing?") asked at code review or QA stage.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If the question arises at code review, the code is already built; validation-based changes are rework. Ask during requirements walkthrough instead.
